title: Lines, Containers, Headings, Paragraphs, Lists, Quotes, and Fenced Code · Language Clinic: Fenced Code
summary: Blank lines erase structure, list markers nest by guesswork, headings skip source evidence, a fence closes with the wrong marker, and paragraphs swallow following blocks. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the fenced code increment in line-oriented block parser with explicit continuation rules. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory…
objectives: Match opening and closing fence character and length, retain info text and literal content, and define end-of-file behavior.
Track list marker, start, indentation, tightness, continuation, quote depth, and blank lines through nested containers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-fenced-code — Match opening and closing fence character and length, retain info text and literal content, and define end-of-file behavior.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-block-parsing-3
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-block-parsing-3.
# Case 5798: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def worked_static_site_block_parsing_3(lines: int = 28, consumed_lines: int = 28, blocks: int = 9, invalid_containers: int = 0, unclosed_fences: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "readerworked3",
        "source5798",
        "parse3dekrow",
        "routeworked35798",
        "artifact5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if lines < 1 or consumed_lines < 0:
        return False, consumed_lines, "line-count"
    if consumed_lines != lines:
        return False, consumed_lines, "line-consumption"
    if blocks < 1 or blocks > lines:
        return False, blocks, "block-shape"
    if invalid_containers > 0 or unclosed_fences > 0:
        return False, invalid_containers + unclosed_fences, "block-boundary"
    assert consumed_lines == lines
    return True, blocks, "bounded-block-tree"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

# Evidence: ssg-fenced-code-7mb9jp
# Competency: ssg-fenced-code.
# Case 3538: a multilingual public-service handbook migration.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.

def evidence_ssg_fenced_code_7mb9jp(lines: int = 28, consumed_lines: int = 28, blocks: int = 9, invalid_containers: int = 0, unclosed_fences: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_7mb9jp = "7mb9jp-pj9bm7-3538"
    assert evidence_variant_7mb9jp
    evidence_axes_7mb9jp = (
        "reader7mb9jp",
        "source3538",
        "parsepj9bm7",
        "route7mb9jp3538",
        "artifact35387mb9jp",
        "recoverypj9bm73538",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_7mb9jp)) == 6
    if lines < 1 or consumed_lines < 0:
        return False, consumed_lines, "line-count"
    if consumed_lines != lines:
        return False, consumed_lines, "line-consumption"
    if blocks < 1 or blocks > lines:
        return False, blocks, "block-shape"
    if invalid_containers > 0 or unclosed_fences > 0:
        return False, invalid_containers + unclosed_fences, "block-boundary"
    assert consumed_lines == lines
    return True, blocks, "bounded-block-tree"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Fenced Code model · Theory Fenced Code
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Fenced Code: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 9805 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to parse headings, paragraphs, containers, and fenced code with explicit line consumption; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Match opening and closing fence character and length, retain info text and literal content, and define end-of-file behavior.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Fenced Code model · Theory Fenced Code.
steps.why: Retrieving Fenced Code through the concrete line-oriented block parser with explicit continuation rules case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Fenced Code model · Theory Fenced Code, keep Fenced Code connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Match opening and closing fence character and length, retain info text and literal content, and define end-of-file behavior.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Fenced Code model · Theory Fenced Code, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Fenced Code precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Fenced Code model · Theory Fenced Code, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 9805 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to parse headings, paragraphs, containers, and fenced code with explicit line consumption; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Match opening and closing fence character and length, retain info text and literal content, and define end-of-file behavior.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Fenced Code model · Theory Fenced Code, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Any three backticks close every fenced code block.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-fenced-code-7mb9jp(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_fenced_code_7mb9jp\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?lines < 1 or consumed_lines < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?consumed_lines != lines)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?blocks < 1 or blocks > lines)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?invalid_containers > 0 or unclosed_fences > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
